title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know
Dull days usually translate to lower rates as fleet efficiencies prevail - Despite the fact that trade volumes remain strong when it comes to major bulks, the absence of any disruption across the major shipping lanes has translated to lower spot freight rates across the spectrum. Read More

Capesize Shipments of Guinea Bauxite Forecasted to Slow Down in Q3 Amid Rainy Seasons
Based on AXSMarine data, during the first five months of 2023, the seaborne export of bauxite remained strong, exhibiting a growth of 2.3 million tons (3.4% year-on-year) compared to the corresponding period of the previous year. Read More

The three things I would tell my clients this week
Last week, central bankers held a conference in Sintra, Portugal.

children.title: The three things I would tell my clients this week
section_path: The three things I would tell my clients this week
children.section_path_text: The three things I would tell my clients this week
children.summary: Last week, central bankers held a conference in Sintra, Portugal. There, they confirmed that inflation remains sticky, and interest rates will thus remain elevated for some time.

children.summary:
The weak economic backdrop continues to weigh on sentiment across the commodity complex. Read More

children.title: Will Iran's Crude/Condensate Exports Return To May Levels?
section_path: Will Iran's Crude/Condensate Exports Return To May Levels?
children.section_path_text: Will Iran's Crude/Condensate Exports Return To May Levels?
children.summary: This insight explores why Iran's crude/condensate export levels observed in May were not sustained, Iran's onshore inventories showed stock draws whilst Shandong inventories built stocks and Iranian floating storage declined.

children.section_path_text: Macro/Geopolitics, Commodity Markets, Freight and Bunker Markets
children.summary:
Following last week's release of China's official manufacturing PMI, which suggested that the country's industrial sector remained in contraction territory for a third consecutive month, the Caixin manufacturing PMI painted a somewhat less negative picture today. Read More

children.title: China's steel production has continued to improve at least
section_path: China's steel production has continued to improve at least
children.section_path_text: China's steel production has continued to improve at least
children.summary:
As we have been stressing in Commodore's Weekly Dry Bulk Reports, China's consumer recovery remains underway and we expect that its industrial recovery will resume this quarter. Read More

children.title: The Big Picture: El Niño: Risks to Dry Bulk Trades
section_path: The Big Picture: El Niño: Risks to Dry Bulk Trades
children.section_path_text: The Big Picture: El Niño: Risks to Dry Bulk Trades
children.summary: There are precious few certainties in dry bulk shipping, so the World Meteorological Organisation (WMO)'s assessment on 4 July of a 90% probability of El Niño (of at least moderate strength) extending into the second half of 2023 gives us enough of a reason to a look at the potential consequences.

children.summary:
